title: passiflora quadrangularis var. sulcata
intro: Passiflora quadrangularis var. sulcata, also known as the giant granadilla or giant passion flower, is a species of flowering plant in the Passifloraceae family. This tropical plant is native to South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative effects. The health benefits of Passiflora quadrangularis var. sulcata include reducing anxiety and insomnia, relieving menstrual cramps and other pain symptoms, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, and are often used to promote relaxation and calmness.
